raw_input: Write a Python function `word_freq(text: str) -> dict[str, int]` that returns a dictionary mapping each distinct word in `text` (lowercased) to its count. A word is a maximal run of ASCII letters and digits — any other character (punctuation, whitespace, unicode, etc.) is a separator. The empty string should return an empty dict.
success_criteria: punctuation is a separator, not part of a word
digits are part of words (e.g. 'python3' is one word)
constraints: no external dependencies (no regex is fine but not required)
